entry.title: How to Set Facets Visibility in CDS Based on a Feature Toggle?
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm working with cds and have a scenario where I want to conditionally show/hide a facet based on a feature toggle. The facet should only be visible in the UI if the feature toggle is ON.

Is there a way to conditionally set field visibility in the .cds file or through annotations, based on a feature toggle? Has anyone implemented something similar and can share best practices or code snippets?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Cloud Economics (aka FinOps) with SAP BTP
entry.content: Further reading ... BTP FinOps: Keeping Track of your Credits - SAP Community

entry.title: ATC Configuration for Custom code analysis
entry.content:
Hi Team,

ATC Configuration: to include the our satellite system in to the ATC Central system for Custom code analysis. Both systems are on HANA database.

Thanks & Regards.

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: ATC Configuration for Custom code analysis
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Thank you for reaching out and providing the details about your ATC configuration query. I understand that you need to include your satellite system into the ATC Central System for custom code analysis.

Recommended Steps and Resources

  1. Remote Code Analysis Setup:

    • I recommend starting with the technical setup guide for configuring remote code analysis in ATC. You can find a comprehensive blog post here: Remote Code Analysis in ATC - One central check system. This guide will walk you through the steps necessary to connect your satellite system (ATS) to the central system (GS4).

  2. ATC in S/4HANA FAQs:

    • We have an extensive FAQ section on ATC in S/4HANA which might address many of your initial questions. You can access it here.

  3. Generating ATC Findings in Excel:

    • If you need to work with ATC findings in an Excel file, this guide will be helpful: Get your ATC findings into an Excel file.

  4. ATC in S/4HANA Public Cloud Configuration:

    • For specific configurations related to ATC in S/4HANA Public Cloud, you can refer to this page: ATC in S/4HANA (Public Cloud).

Identifying Object Blacklist & Whitelist and Relevant SAP Notes

To identify the object blacklists and whitelists and relevant SAP notes for including the satellite system (ATS) into the central system (GS4), you can follow these steps:

  1. Object Blacklist & Whitelist:

    • Refer to the SAP Community for recommendations on managing object blacklists and whitelists. You can also find specific details in the ATC in S/4HANA FAQs.

  2. Relevant SAP Notes:

    • SAP Notes for ATC configuration are regularly updated. You can search for the latest SAP notes on the SAP Support Portal using keywords like "ATC S/4HANA configuration" and "ATS integration".

I hope this information will help you with smooth integration of your satellite system into the central ATC system.

Kind Regards,
Luiza
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Cloud Economics (aka FinOps) with SAP BTP
entry.content: Furhter further reading ... SAP Discovery Center Mission - Control your Operational Cost: Setting up a BTP Financial Operations application

entry.title: Discrepancy in planned effort in "Plan Customer Projects" and C_EngmntProjFcstQuery_2
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We see some discrepancy in planned effort of Work Package and planned effort value in C_EngmntProjFcstQuery_2  query.

For example, planned effort for month of May 2025 is 100 hours but in the query we are having the value as 90 hours.

Thanks


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Discrepancy in planned effort in "Plan Customer Proje...
entry.content:
Hello,

The 90 Hours for Jan 2025 is coming from the Effort (Staffed) rather than Effort (Planned). This is the intended behaviour for C_EngmntProjFcstQuery_2 when there is a Staffed Effort. Please review the distribution under Effort (Staffed) in Plan Customer Projects to see where the values are being pulled from.

Best regards,

SAP Support
